title: FIRST CONTACT — LIFELINE CUT V5
episodeObjective: Establish first contact with Ethan Hale, guide him to the relay room, stabilize the connection, recover Door 12 evidence, and end with the first coherent contradiction around Hale's memory.
rulesApplied: Characters report lived events, not game systems
Every visible choice has exactly two options
Physical movement uses characterBusy with hidden wait time
Return nodes describe what happened while the character was gone
No meaningless fragments or awkward translation-style metaphors
Route choices are not labelled safe/dangerous directly
Lore is delivered through lived experience and recovered evidence
Hale can be uncertain, defensive, tired, sarcastic, and wrong

relay_password.title: RELAY ACCESS
relay_password.description: Access code required. Use the active relay authorization, not the deprecated fragment.

power_routing_01.title: RELAY POWER ROUTING
power_routing_01.description: Legacy matching puzzle preserved for compatibility. V5 replaces this with a two-choice relay power decision.

events.text: [UNKNOWN RELAY SIGNAL DETECTED]
Hello?
If someone is there, answer me.
One letter is enough.
Wait.
You answered.
Okay. Okay, that's real.
My name is Ethan Hale. I'm on ECHO Station.
I'm wedged in a service alcove beside a relay corridor. Emergency strip on the floor. No ceiling lights.
Something behind the wall keeps ticking every few seconds.
It did not do that before.
Dr. Ethan Hale. Chief engineer.
I used to keep the relay spine, power buses, and life-support routing from killing each other.
It was a boring job when the station behaved.
I miss boring.
Fair.
Annoying, but fair.
I'm sending suit telemetry. If this is fake, it is a very committed fake with bad oxygen numbers.
[BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT ATTACHED]

events.title: BIOMETRIC FRAGMENT: HALE
events.content:
SUBJECT: ETHAN HALE
HEART RATE: ELEVATED
OXYGEN: 32%
SUIT LINK: UNSTABLE
VOICEPRINT MATCH: 81%
events.caption: Partial telemetry fragment transmitted through the relay.

events.text: Oxygen is at thirty-two percent.
The suit says battery is forty. I don't trust it enough to feel comforted.
Something hit the station hard.
Lights went first. Then internal comms. Then the relay started throwing old voices back at me.
Recordings. Loops. Distress calls that ended months ago.
You are the first response that feels like a person.

choices.text: TELL ME WHAT YOU CAN SEE.

events.text: Good.
I needed that more than I wanted to.
But don't trust me blindly. I'm tired, low on air, and there are gaps in my memory.
If I sound too sure about something, make me prove it.
Narrow corridor. Cable trays on the left wall. Some of them melted through their brackets.
Floor is covered in gray dust. My boots leave clean marks in it.
There are older footprints too.
Same direction I'm going.
I don't love that.
Relay room is past the dark stretch of corridor.
The door needs my maintenance authorization. I can still pull one damaged crew record from the cache.

events.title: CREW LOG: ETHAN HALE
events.content:
DR. ETHAN HALE — CHIEF ENGINEER
RELAY MAINTENANCE AUTHORIZATION: 73_1
STATUS: ACTIVE

NOTE: Secondary maintenance index lists deprecated fragment: _319.
Current authorization must be reconstructed from active record.
events.caption: Recovered identity and maintenance fragment. Authorization appears partially damaged.

events.text: The active line is damaged. The old fragment is still visible.
Don't copy the old fragment whole. It is marked deprecated for a reason.
I'm going to the relay door now.
I need both hands free for the corridor. If I keep talking, I'll stop listening.
I'm at the door.
Took longer than it should have. Half the corridor is blocked by fallen insulation panels.
The ticking stopped when I passed the third service hatch.
I don't know if that is better.
Access panel is alive. Keypad cracked, but the first four digits still respond.
[LOCKED DEVICE DETECTED: RELAY ACCESS]
[ACCESS DENIED]
Stop. Don't try another random number.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The panel noticed the failed attempt. I can hear the lock cycling inside the frame.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Look at the record again. Active authorization: seventy-three, blank, one. Deprecated fragment: three-one-nine.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Only one digit fits the damaged spot.
events.speaker: HALE

choices.text: SLOW DOWN. WALK ME THROUGH IT.

events.text: All right. Slowly.
The current code is not hidden like a riddle. It is damaged like a bad transmission.
Seventy-three. Missing digit. One.
The old record shows three-one-nine. The system says not to use the whole old record.
So we borrow the part that fits.
[RELAY PANEL AWAITING INPUT]

choices.text: ASK HALE TO GUESS IT HIMSELF.

events.text: I could guess.
I could also put my hand in a live junction box and hope it appreciates confidence.
Use the record. My memory is not better than the evidence right now.
[ACCESS GRANTED]
Door opened.
The room is colder than the corridor.
Three relay columns. One dark. One humming low. One pulsing like a heartbeat with bad timing.
I need to stabilize the link from inside.
I can keep reserve power on life support and push the relay through a dirty line.
Or I can clean up our signal by borrowing from reserve power.
One keeps me safer. One keeps you closer.

choices.text: KEEP RESERVE POWER ON LIFE SUPPORT.

events.text: Good. Reserve stays with life support.
I am annoyingly attached to breathing.
I'll force the relay through the dirtier line. It will sound ugly, but ugly is acceptable.
Borrowing reserve for the relay.
That gives us a cleaner carrier. It also makes my oxygen margin worse.
I understand why you chose it. I just want the cost said out loud.
[RELAY STABILIZED]
I'm back.
The relay is holding. Rough, but holding.
Floor vibrates when the dirty line surges. Feels like standing on a sleeping animal.
Life support stayed clean. My suit alarm finally stopped scolding me.
There is a security cache behind the second relay column.
I remember installing it.
I don't remember why I suddenly hate looking at it.
[RELAY STABILIZED]
I'm back.
Your signal is clearer now. I can actually hear the silence between words.
My suit alarm hated the power dip. It made a sound I would describe as personally disappointed.
There is a security cache behind the second relay column.
I remember putting it there.
That memory comes with a bad feeling.
The cache panel is under a burned insulation flap.
I need to step away from the console and open it manually.
If I leave the relay console alone, the carrier may drift.
If I ignore the cache, we lose the first real clue we've found.
Opening the cache.
I'm stepping away from the relay console. If the line warbles, don't panic.
Observer warning.
I was hoping you missed that.
Something is sitting on the edge of the carrier.
Not talking yet. Just listening.
Like a person standing behind you and not breathing.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK HIM ABOUT DOOR 12
There. It heard us notice it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm opening the cache now. Before it decides what we get to know.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I'm back.
Cache was stuck. I had to pry the panel with a broken bracket.
There is a sealed evidence packet inside.
Label says Door 12.
My hand went cold when I read it.
I'm opening it.
I'm back.
The cache panel was warm. It should not be warm.
Something scratched the inside of the insulation flap.
I don't know how something scratches from inside a wall panel.
The packet is labelled Door 12.
The carrier just got quieter.
I am opening it anyway.
[SECURITY CACHE RECOVERED]

events.title: SECURITY LOG
events.content:
21:17 — Door 12 opened.
21:17 — Manual override accepted.
21:18 — Operator identity redacted.
21:20 — Internal sensor flagged decompression risk.
events.caption: Recovered security event from the station archive.

events.text: [MEDICAL CACHE RECOVERED]

events.title: MEDICAL REPORT
events.content:
21:20 — Casualty reported.
Cause: decompression trauma.
Location: Door 12 access corridor.
Status: fatal.
events.caption: Recovered emergency medical report.

events.title: CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT
events.content:
SOURCE: DOOR 12 SECURITY FEED
FRAME STATUS: VISUAL DATA CORRUPTED
ANALYSIS RESULT:
- One subject detected outside Door 12.
- Subject biometric outline matches Ethan Hale.
- Object detected in subject's right hand: manual override handle.
- Frame terminated before decompression event.
events.caption: Automated analysis generated from corrupted visual feed. No image recovered.
events.message: [CAMERA ANALYSIS REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: That's my outline.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Outside Door 12.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Holding the manual override.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I don't remember this.
events.speaker: HALE

choices.text: DO YOU REMEMBER ANY OF THIS?

events.text: I can't explain something I don't remember doing.
I know how that sounds.
I know exactly how weak it is.
But if I invent a cleaner answer, we both get a nicer lie and a worse chance of surviving.
[HALE COOPERATION DEGRADED]
Not clearly.
I remember red light over a door.
I remember someone shouting my name from behind me.
Not angry. Terrified.
My hand was near the override handle.
Then the memory cuts off.
Not fades. Cuts.

event.speaker: UNKNOWN
event.text: ASK HIM WHAT HAPPENED AFTER THE DOOR OPENED

events.text: The channel pulled away from me.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That was not random interference.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Something pressed into the carrier while we were reading the files.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I need to seal the evidence packet before the cache corrupts it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: [RECORD PACKAGE SEALED]
Good. Evidence is sealed.
Security log. Medical report. Camera analysis.
All preserved.
Operator, if Door 12 comes up again, don't let me step around it.
I don't trust my memory enough to protect either of us from it.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: HE OPENED IT

choices.text: TELL HALE TO HOLD POSITION FIRST.

events.text: [CHANNEL HELD OPEN]

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: DO NOT CLOSE THE RECORD
You kept it open.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I understand why.
Curiosity feels like courage until something answers back.

events.speaker: UNKNOWN
events.text: ASK WHO WAS OUTSIDE DOOR 12
We're leaving the relay room before it learns anything else from us.
events.speaker: HALE

majorChanges: Rebuilt dialogue to feel like real-time messaging instead of report-style exposition.
Removed awkward lines such as relay corpse, ghost mouth, and one-word fragments.
Added characterBusy events and return nodes for hidden wait-time gameplay.
Converted relay power routing from matching puzzle into a meaningful two-choice survival tradeoff.
Kept the same core scenario: first contact, relay access, Door 12 evidence, Unknown Signal, Episode 02 transition.
Every choice group now has exactly two choices.
Hale reports what happened while he was unavailable instead of returning with empty lines.
Unknown signal appears earlier and reacts differently based on player attention.
